activities_projects.title: Detailed Character Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the behaviour and personality of the child
steps: Read the entire story carefully
Write a detailed character sketch of the child in 6–7 sentences

activities_projects.title: Symbolism in the Story
activities_projects.objective: Identify symbolic meanings in the story
steps: Identify objects like sweets, balloons, flowers and roundabout
Explain how they represent the child’s desires

activities_projects.title: Psychological State of the Child
activities_projects.objective: Understand the psychological emotions of the child
steps: Read the section where the child realizes he is lost
Explain his fear, anxiety and helplessness

activities_projects.title: Contrast Between Joy and Fear
steps: Describe the joyful beginning of the story
Explain how it changes into fear later

activities_projects.title: Narrative Technique
activities_projects.objective: Understand how the author narrates the story
steps: Identify how the author uses description and dialogue
Explain how it makes the story interesting

activities_projects.title: Importance of Parents
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the importance of parents in the story
steps: Explain why the child finally wants only his parents
Write 5–6 sentences explaining their importance

activities_projects.title: Role of the Stranger
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the role of the man who helps the child
steps: Describe how the man tries to comfort the child
Explain why his attempts fail

activities_projects.title: Theme Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the main message of the story
steps: Identify the central theme of the story
Explain how the author conveys it

activities_projects.title: Alternative Ending
steps: Imagine what happens after the story ends
Write a possible ending in 6–7 sentences

activities_projects.title: Life Lesson Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyse the life lesson from the story
steps: Think about what the story teaches readers
Explain the lesson in 6–7 sentences
